title: H5N1 Avian Influenza — European Poultry Outbreaks 2026
summary: Rapidly evolving H5N1 situation in European poultry with confirmed cases moving closer to Swiss borders. Wild bird detections along the Rhine corridor suggest ongoing introduction via migratory routes. German case near Basel region is of immediate concern for Swiss poultry farms. Austrian suspected case in Vorarlberg adds a third border front.
swiss_impact_assessment: HIGH — H5N1 clade 2.3.4.4b detected in commercial poultry in Germany (Baden-Württemberg) and wild birds in France along the Rhine. Suspected case in Austrian Vorarlberg near Lake Constance. Swiss poultry sector at direct risk on multiple borders. BLV should consider heightened biosecurity measures and enhanced wild bird surveillance in northwestern and northeastern Switzerland.
recommended_actions: Alert cantonal veterinary offices in BS, BL, AG, ZH, SG, TG
Increase wild bird surveillance along Rhine and Lake Constance
Review poultry farm biosecurity compliance in border cantons
Coordinate with German, French, and Austrian veterinary authorities
Pre-position H5N1 test kits at cantonal labs
Issue guidance to poultry farmers on enhanced biosecurity measures
Consider mandatory housing orders for free-range poultry in high-risk zones
human_health_status: No human cases in Europe. Low risk of human infection with current clade 2.3.4.4b.
animal_health_status: CRITICAL — Active outbreaks in NL, DE, FR poultry. Suspected case in AT. Wild bird die-offs reported along Rhine corridor.
environmental_status: Wild bird migration actively spreading virus along European flyways. Rhine corridor and Lake Constance identified as high-risk zones.
